# SDK Requirements Quality Checklist: MeshLink Offline BLE Mesh SDK

**Purpose**: Validate whether the MeshLink SDK requirements are complete, clear,
consistent, measurable, and ready for peer review before planning.
**Created**: 2026-05-10
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based
on the current specification only. `plan.md` and `tasks.md` are not present, so
this checklist intentionally tests spec quality rather than planning quality.

## Requirement Completeness

- [X] CHK001 Are first-contact trust requirements complete for initial
  acceptance, subsequent verification, and trust reset or revocation handoff?
  [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-003, Spec §Assumptions]
- [X] CHK002 Does the spec define the bounded retry window with enough detail to
  distinguish expiry from indefinite retry? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007, Spec §Edge
  Cases]
- [X] CHK003 Are requirements specified for how route changes during an active
  transfer affect an in-flight message? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §Message
  Transfer]

## Requirement Clarity

- [X] CHK004 Is "bounded local window" quantified with a duration, attempt
  count, or both? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-007]
- [X] CHK005 Is "first successful contact" defined clearly enough to know when
  TOFU trust is persisted? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]
- [X] CHK006 Is "best-effort message delivery" in low-power mode clarified with
  explicit expectations or exclusions? [Ambiguity, Spec §User Story 3, Spec
  §SC-006]

## Requirement Consistency

- [X] CHK007 Are trust-related terms (`trusted`, `untrusted`,
  `identity-changed`, `trust failure`) used consistently across stories, edge
  cases, requirements, and assumptions? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 1, Spec
  §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-002–FR-011, Spec §Assumptions]
- [X] CHK008 Are restart expectations consistent between retry loss, stale
  state rebuild, and resumed trusted messaging? [Consistency, Spec §User Story
  1, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-008, Spec §Assumptions]
- [X] CHK009 Are oversize-payload requirements consistent between edge cases,
  functional requirements, assumptions, and success criteria? [Consistency,
  Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-009, Spec §SC-003, Spec §Assumptions]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [X] CHK010 Can the first-message integration criterion be objectively
  evaluated without hidden prerequisites or undocumented setup assumptions?
  [Measurability, Spec §SC-001, Spec §Assumptions]
- [X] CHK011 Are mesh recovery and delivery criteria measurable for temporary
  route loss, retry expiry, and failure outcomes? [Measurability, Spec
  §SC-002, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-011]
- [X] CHK012 Are cross-platform parity criteria measurable beyond broad phrases
  like "equivalent workflows" and "same meanings"? [Clarity, Spec §SC-007,
  Spec §FR-014]

## Scenario Coverage

- [X] CHK013 Are requirements defined for the primary happy path, first-contact
  trust path, retry or recovery path, and restart recovery path? [Coverage,
  Spec §User Stories 1–3, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [X] CHK014 Does the spec define what happens when a trusted peer is explicitly
  revoked and later reappears? [Gap, Spec §FR-003, Spec §Assumptions]
- [X] CHK015 Are requirements specified for mixed-platform mesh participation
  beyond lifecycle and diagnostic parity? [Gap, Spec §User Story 3, Spec
  §FR-014, Spec §SC-007]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [X] CHK016 Are requirements defined for route changes during an active large
  transfer? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [X] CHK017 Are requirements defined for duplicate, partial, or out-of-order
  acknowledgements, not just chunks? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-010]
- [X] CHK018 Are battery-tier transition requirements defined for mid-transfer
  or mid-session state changes? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-013, Spec
  §SC-006]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [X] CHK019 Are privacy and local-storage requirements defined for persisted
  TOFU trust records and diagnostic data? [Gap, Spec §FR-002, Spec §Key
  Entities, Spec §Constitutional Alignment]
- [X] CHK020 Are observability requirements defined clearly enough to know which
  delivery, trust, routing, and power events must be surfaced? [Gap, Spec
  §FR-011, Spec §Key Entities]
- [X] CHK021 Are performance targets aligned with the stated scale assumptions
  of 8 peers, 64 KB transfers, and no retry persistence across restart?
  [Consistency, Spec §SC-003–SC-006, Spec §Assumptions]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [X] CHK022 Are host-application responsibilities clearly separated from SDK
  responsibilities for trust UX, recipient selection, and message resubmission
  after restart? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002–FR-003, Spec §FR-008, Spec
  §Assumptions]
- [X] CHK023 Does the spec validate the assumption that required BLE and
  background permissions are available when evaluating offline-only success
  criteria? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §SC-001–SC-007]
- [X] CHK024 Are sample-app and supported-device assumptions explicit enough for
  a reviewer to judge whether SC-001 and SC-004 are fairly testable?
  [Assumption, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-004, Spec §Assumptions]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [X] CHK025 Does the spec distinguish clearly between "unreachable peer",
  "expired retry window", and "trust failure" as separate outcome classes?
  [Clarity, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-011]
- [X] CHK026 Is the canonical term for peer state defined clearly enough that
  reviewers know when to use `trusted`, `untrusted`, `revoked`, or
  `identity-changed`? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec
  §Assumptions]
- [X] CHK027 Do the requirements imply any conflict between "serverless"
  behavior and host-app-managed trust UX or message resubmission
  responsibilities? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001–FR-003, Spec §FR-008, Spec §Out
  of Scope, Spec §Assumptions]

## Notes

- Audience: PR reviewer
- Depth: Standard
- Focus: Full balanced SDK coverage across security, routing, transfer,
  performance, parity, and assumption quality
- This checklist intentionally tests requirement quality only; it is not an
  implementation or QA verification checklist.
